key_insight: Atmospheric Plasma Spray (APS) coatings can significantly improve the wear resistance and surface durability of agricultural plough parts operating in abrasive soil conditions.
engineering_relevance: The study supports APS as a practical surface-engineering route for extending plough life, reducing downtime, and reconditioning worn components.
method: Applied APS thermal coatings to active plough components exposed to soil contact.
Evaluated coating performance using hardness testing and microscratch analysis.
Examined the coated surface microstructure with scanning electron microscopy (SEM).
evidence_signal: The abstract reports coating hardness of about 204 HV and microscratch behavior with a coefficient of friction greater than 1, alongside SEM-based surface characterization.
watch_item: Results are based on experimental coating characterization, so field durability, adhesion robustness, and long-term wear performance should still be validated under real tillage conditions.
paper_title: Improving the Microstructural and Mechanical Properties of Agricultural Ploughs Through Atmospheric Plasma Spray (APS) Thermal Coatings
